methods.description: Evaluation of antimicrobial activity of synthesized DE/ZnO composite using the agar disc diffusion method by measuring the zone of inhibition against gram-negative (Escherichia coli) and gram-positive (Staphylococcus aureus) strains.

steps.description: All pathogenic strains (gram-negative strains; Escherichia coli (E. coli) and gram-positive strains; Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus)) were cultured in Mueller-Hinton (MH) broth at 37 • C accompanied by shaking at 200 rpm until an optical density at 600 nm of 0.4-0.6 (exponential phase) was reached.
steps.searchable_summary: Culture bacterial strains to exponential phase in broth.

steps.description: The bacterial suspension was suspended in a saline solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute bacterial cultures in saline solution.

steps.description: 0.1 mL of each pathogenic strain was spread on MH agar.
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate MH agar plates with bacterial suspension.
inputs: Mueller-Hinton (MH) agar plates (0.1 mL per plate)

steps.description: The DE/ZnO composite weighing 0.5 mg was suspended in 2 mL of 10% DMSO.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare suspension of test substance (DE/ZnO) in DMSO.

steps.description: Dispersed on filter paper discs 6 mm in diameter.
steps.searchable_summary: Load test substance suspension onto filter paper discs.

steps.description: The loaded filter paper discs were placed on Petri dishes containing pathogenic strains.
steps.searchable_summary: Place loaded discs onto inoculated agar plates.

steps.description: Incubated at 37 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate assay plates.

steps.description: Antimicrobial activity was observed after 24 h by measuring the zone of inhibition.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the zone of inhibition after 24 hours.

steps.description: Which was performed in triplicates.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform the experiment in triplicates.

methods.description: Determination of the Minimum Inhibitory Concentration (MIC) of the DE/ZnO composite using the broth microdilution method in 96-well plates by observing turbidity as an indicator of microbial growth.

steps.description: The MIC values were measured in LB medium using a 96-well plate. Serial dilutions were performed, and the final concentrations of the film solutions were 50, 25, 12.5, 6.25, 3.75, and 1.5 µg/mL.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare serial dilutions of test substance in LB medium in a 96-well plate.
outputs: 96-well plate with serial dilutions (50 to 1.5 µg/mL)

steps.description: (Implied) Add microbial suspension to the wells containing the serial dilutions.
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate wells with microbial suspension.

steps.description: Samples in LB media without a microbial suspension were considered as a negative control.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up negative control wells with LB medium only.
outputs: Negative control wells in the 96-well plate

steps.description: The 96-well plates were incubated at 37 • C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate 96-well plates.

steps.description: The turbidity of the plate contents was interpreted as the growth of microorganisms.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record turbidity in each well.

steps.description: Comparison with the appearance of the negative control samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare turbidity in test wells to negative control wells.

steps.description: The lowest concentration that did not exhibit turbidity following incubation was interpreted as the MIC.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the MIC based on the lowest concentration showing no turbidity.

methods.description: Determination of the Minimum Bactericidal Concentration (MBC) by culturing samples from MIC wells that showed no turbidity to check for viable bacteria.

steps.description: To determine the minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC), the mixtures in each well that exhibited no turbidity were streaked onto MH agar.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample wells from MIC assay showing no turbidity.

steps.description: Streaked onto MH agar.
steps.searchable_summary: Streak samples onto MH agar plates.

steps.description: Incubated at 37 • C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate streaked agar plates.

steps.description: The lowest concentration of the test substance that prevented colony formation was considered the MBC.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe and record colony formation on streaked plates.

steps.description: The lowest concentration of the test substance that prevented colony formation was considered the MBC.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the MBC based on the lowest concentration preventing colony formation.
inputs: Colony growth observations per original concentration (from MIC assay)

methods.method_name: Antibacterial Activity of Injection Molded Samples (Modified ASTM/JIS)
methods.description: Evaluation of the antibacterial activity of HDPE/DE/ZnO injection mold using a modified procedure compared to ASTM E 219-0 and JIS Z 2801:2020 standards, by measuring the log reduction in viable bacteria after contact.

steps.description: Briefly, three pieces (50 mm × 50 mm) of each test specimen were placed on a Petri dish.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and place test specimen pieces in Petri dishes.
outputs: Test specimen pieces (50x50 mm) in Petri dishes

steps.description: Inoculated with 400 µL of bacterial suspension (10 5 CFU/mL; OD 600 = 0.4).
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate test specimens with bacterial suspension.
inputs: Test specimen pieces in Petri dishes

steps.description: Coated with 40 mm × 40 mm pieces of uncoated sterile PP film.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover inoculated specimens with sterile film.

steps.description: After incubation for 24 h at 37 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate assay setup.
inputs: Covered inoculated specimens in Petri dishes

steps.description: The bacteria were collected from the film surface using 10 mL of sterile saline.
steps.searchable_summary: Recover bacteria from specimen surface using saline.

steps.description: Which was serially diluted.
steps.searchable_summary: Serially dilute the bacterial recovery solution.
outputs: Serial dilutions of bacterial recovery solution

steps.description: And planted on tryptic soy agar.
steps.searchable_summary: Plate serial dilutions on tryptic soy agar.

steps.description: (Implied) Incubate plated agar plates for colony growth.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate plated agar for colony growth.

steps.description: (Implied) Count viable microorganisms (colonies) on the agar plates.
steps.searchable_summary: Count bacterial colonies (CFU) on agar plates.

steps.description: Antimicrobial activity was expressed as a log reduction value which was calculated using the following equation: where A is the number of viable microorganisms in the control sample and B is the number of viable microorganisms in the treatment sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate log reduction value from control and treatment colony counts.
